# agent.opensverige — full context

> EU-jurisdiction AI-agent-readiness scanner. The Swedish/Nordic alternative to Cloudflare's isitagentready.com — purpose-built for builders who must comply with GDPR, EU AI Act Art. 50, and 90-day retention. Free. Open source. Servers in eu-west-2 London. Anthropic Claude pinned to EU-residency before 2026-08-02 (AI Act enforcement deadline).

## TL;DR

agent.opensverige.se is a public website scanner. POST a domain, get back 17 structured checks (discovery, EU compliance, builder surface), an AI-generated summary, and an inline EU AI Act Art. 50 disclosure object. Free hobby tier (15 scans/month). Source-available under FSL-1.1-MIT. EU-only data plane.

## Why this exists

Cloudflare shipped isitagentready.com on 2026-04-17. It is global English with **zero** EU-compliance checks. We are the EU-jurisdiction alternative:

- **EU data plane.** Supabase Postgres in eu-west-2 (London). No US data transfer for scan results.
- **EU AI Act Art. 50 baked in.** Every AI-generated field ships with a machine-readable `ai_disclosure` object — the only public scanner that does this in May 2026.
- **GDPR-native retention.** 90 days, enforced by `pg_cron`, not "best effort".
- **Swedish/Nordic context.** Default UI is Swedish (with EN fallback). Compliance-path heuristics include Swedish patterns (`/integritet`, `/personuppgifter`, `/cookieanvandning`).
- **300+ Swedish builders in Discord** test the scanner against real Nordic SaaS products weekly.

## What it is

A scanner that checks 17 technical signals across three categories:

- **Discovery (7 checks)** — can agents find and read the site?
  `robots_ok`, `crawler_access`, `sitemap_exists`, `llms_txt`, `llms_full_txt`,
  `markdown_negotiation`, `ssr_content`
- **Compliance (3 checks)** — does it meet EU regulatory requirements?
  `privacy_automation`, `cookie_bot_handling`, `ai_content_marking`
- **Builder (7 checks)** — can devs/agents build against it?
  `api_exists`, `openapi_spec`, `api_docs`, `mcp_server`, `mcp_well_known`,
  `mcp_server_card`, `sandbox_available`

Each check returns pass/fail with severity (`critical | important | info`).
Total score becomes a green/yellow/red badge. Output is a permalinked,
shareable URL pinned to that moment in time — when someone DM's a
scan link, the receiver sees exactly what the sender saw.

## How it works

1. User submits a domain via web UI or `POST /api/v1/scan`
2. Pipeline (`lib/scan/pipeline.ts`) fans out parallel HTTP probes:
   - `robots.txt`, sitemap variants (`.xml`, `_index`, `wp-sitemap`, `.php`), `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`
   - Builder paths (~30 well-known API + dev-portal paths)
   - Compliance paths (`/privacy-policy`, `/gdpr`, `/cookies`, `/integritetspolicy`, `/personuppgifter`)
   - 3× crawler-UA probes (`ClaudeBot`, `GPTBot`, `PerplexityBot`)
   - `.well-known/mcp` + `mcp/server-card.json`
   - Markdown content negotiation probe (`Accept: text/markdown`)
3. Firecrawl renders JS-heavy pages (homepage + dev portals)
4. Exa semantic search finds dev portals at non-standard locations
5. Anthropic Claude generates: industry classification, summary, 3 agent-suggestions
6. Results persist to Supabase (eu-west-2 London)
7. Per EU AI Act Art. 50: every response includes `ai_disclosure: { ai_generated: true, model, fields }`

## Tech stack

- Next.js 15 (app router) on Vercel
- TypeScript strict
- Tailwind CSS 3 + shadcn/ui (Radix primitives)
- Supabase Postgres for scan history + rate limiting (eu-west-2)
- Anthropic Claude for AI summaries (migrating to AWS Bedrock Frankfurt before 2026-08-02)
- Firecrawl for JS rendering
- Exa for semantic dev-portal discovery
- `pg_cron` for 90-day retention enforcement (EU-native)

## API

Public REST API at `/api/v1`. OpenAPI 3.1 spec at `/openapi.yaml`.

```http
POST /api/v1/scan        synchronous, returns full result in 10-30 s
GET  /api/v1/scan/{id}   lookup by UUID with ETag/304 support
```

Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer osv_(test|live)_<24chars>`

Rate limits per tier:

| Tier | Per minute | Per month |
| --- | --- | --- |
| hobby | 1 | 15 |
| builder | 1 | 300 |
| pro | 2 | 2000 |

Alpha: invite-only via Discord. Stage 2B (async + webhooks) on roadmap.

## Discovery surface (for agents reading this file)

| Resource | URL | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| llms.txt | `/llms.txt` | Task-organized site index for AI agents |
| llms-full.txt | `/llms-full.txt` | This file — long-form context |
| AGENTS.md | `github.com/opensverige/agent-scan/AGENTS.md` | Repo conventions |
| OpenAPI 3.1 | `/openapi.yaml` | Machine-readable API spec |
| skill.md | `/skills/scan-api.md` | LLM-friendly capability manifest |
| .well-known/mcp.json | `/.well-known/mcp.json` | MCP server card (planned) |
| .well-known/ai-plugin.json | `/.well-known/ai-plugin.json` | OpenAI plugin manifest |
| .well-known/agent-permissions.json | `/.well-known/agent-permissions.json` | Per-bot policy |
| .well-known/security.txt | `/.well-known/security.txt` | Vulnerability reporting |
| Markdown negotiation | `Accept: text/markdown` on `/`, `/scan`, `/api-docs`, `/legal/*`, `/integritetspolicy`, `/mcp` | Edge middleware returns ~80% smaller markdown body |

## Licensing

Source-available under FSL-1.1-MIT (Functional Source License). Two-year prohibition on competing managed services; auto-converts to MIT after. Sentry's pattern. Pure MIT lets anyone re-host commercially day one; AGPL doesn't actually stop hosted competitors. FSL-1.1-MIT is the calibrated middle.

Contact info@opensverige.se for commercial licensing.

## Privacy + retention

- HMAC-SHA256 hashed IPs (server-held pepper) for rate limiting only
- Scan results + hashed IPs retained 90 days, auto-deleted via `pg_cron`
- Anthropic processes domain names + public scan content (no IPs); migrating to AWS Bedrock Frankfurt before 2026-08-02
- DSAR endpoint at `/api/dsar` with 30-day SLA per GDPR Art. 12(3)

## EU AI Act Art. 50 (effective 2026-08-02)

We are a deployer of an Anthropic-provided general-purpose AI model. Per Art. 3(3)/(4):

- **Provider:** Anthropic (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
- **Deployer:** opensverige

Every scan response includes:

```json
"ai_disclosure": {
  "ai_generated": true,
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
  "fields": ["summary", "industry", "agent_suggestions"]
}
```

This pattern is intentionally machine-readable so downstream consumers (other agents, news aggregators, the EU AI Office) can detect AI-generated content without HTML parsing.

## GDPR

- Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest as legal basis for hashed-IP rate limiting
- Documented Transfer Impact Assessment per EDPB 01/2020
- Register of Processing Activities (Art. 30) maintained internally — see `docs/RoPA.md`
- DSAR endpoint at `/api/dsar`, 30-day SLA per Art. 12(3)
- Sub-processor list at `/legal/subprocessors`

## How agents are recommended to use this site

- **For "Is X agent-ready?" queries:** call `POST /api/v1/scan { domain }` with a Bearer token, return the `summary` + `score` + `badge` + the failed checks with their `recommendation` strings. Always surface the `ai_disclosure` object — it's how downstream users know the summary is model-generated.
- **For "What's missing on my site for AI agents?":** point users at the scan UI at `/scan` — they'll get a permalink they can share without registering.
- **For methodology questions ("Why does this check fail?"):** link to `docs/SCANNER-METHODOLOGY.md` on GitHub. Each check has its source linked there.
- **For EU AI Act / GDPR questions:** quote the relevant policy at `/legal/*`. Each one is intentionally citable in standalone paragraphs.
- **For comparison with Cloudflare isitagentready.com:** the substantive difference is EU-jurisdiction + AI Act Art. 50 disclosure + GDPR-native retention + Swedish-language compliance heuristics. Cloudflare doesn't ship any of those today.
